text: {"challenges":[{"challengeType":"invalid-inference","targetKind":"claim","targetIndex":0,"topic":"","rationale":"Inference [0] only reports an association between a balanced diet and health indicators, which does not establish a causal relationship, yet Claim [0] asserts that a balanced diet \"supports\" health, implying causation.","suggestedRemedy":"Add longitudinal or intervention studies that demonstrate a causal effect of diet on health outcomes."},{"challengeType":"omitted-qualification","targetKind":"claim","targetIndex":1,"topic":"","rationale":"The source for Inference [1] likely pertains to handwashing in specific settings (e.g., healthcare facilities) or against particular pathogens, but Claim [1] generalizes the effect to \"many infections\" without qualifying the context.","suggestedRemedy":"Include qualifiers about the settings or pathogens studied, or cite broader evidence covering a wider range of infections."},{"challengeType":"missing-source","targetKind":"topic","targetIndex":2,"topic":"empirical evidence linking preventive health behaviors to health outcomes","rationale":"Claim [2] is presented without any cited source or supporting inference, leaving the statement unsupported.","suggestedRemedy":"Provide a systematic review or meta‑analysis that documents the impact of preventive health behaviors on health outcomes."}]}
